name: Tiny Startups
description: A directory and community spotlighting tiny startups and maker projects. The audience is indie hackers, solo founders and early adopters who like scrappy, bootstrapped products.

auth.notes: Create an account with email/password (social sign-in often available). Submissions are reviewed before being featured; a paid tier can fast-track or boost a listing.

best_practices: Lead with the 'tiny' angle — a small, focused product built by a maker resonates more than an enterprise pitch.
Keep the tagline to one punchy, jargon-free line that names the user and the outcome.
Tell the founder/maker story briefly in the description; this community connects with the person behind the build.
Use a clean square logo that reads well at thumbnail size in the directory grid.
Add your Twitter/X handle so makers can follow and amplify your launch.
Make sure the link goes to a live, working page — broken or coming-soon links get rejected.

questions.label: Tagline
questions.help: One short line: who it's for and what it does.

questions.label: Description
questions.help: What it does, who it's for, and the maker story in a couple of sentences.
